jokes.text: 256? [Groans] And I'm stuck with this useless 252.
jokes.setup: Gamestation 256 is advertised as the latest gaming system
jokes.punchline: Bart's current 252 system is instantly rendered obsolete by just 4 units
jokes.explanation: Classic tech consumer frustration - the arbitrary nature of gaming system numbering makes older models instantly worthless

jokes.text: Don't destroy me. I can still make you happy... to the max! Here's a headline for Jay. 'Ketchup Truck Hits Hamburger Stand.' [Laughs]
jokes.setup: Gaming system pleads for its life by offering entertainment
jokes.punchline: The 'entertainment' is a terrible Jay Leno-style headline joke
jokes.explanation: Multi-layered: anthropomorphizing the game system, mocking bad late-night TV jokes, and the desperation of outdated technology

jokes.text: Mom, can I have 200 bucks for a 256-K Gamestation? That's less than a dollar a K.
jokes.setup: Bart asking for expensive gaming system
jokes.punchline: His attempt to make $200 sound reasonable by breaking it down per 'K'
jokes.explanation: Bart's flawed kid logic trying to make an expensive purchase seem reasonable through meaningless math

jokes.text: Marge's thrift song about putting money in your cap, interrupted by 'I don't have a cap.'
jokes.setup: Marge launches into an elaborate song about saving money
jokes.punchline: Bart's literal-minded objection deflates the entire musical number
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Marge's earnest musical approach vs Bart's practical literalism. The interruption structure works beautifully.

jokes.text: You want money? Get a job like your old man. / So now you're smarter than your old man, huh? / I guess. / I like your attitude. Take what you need.
jokes.setup: Homer challenges Bart to get a job, then gets defensive when Bart agrees he's smarter
jokes.punchline: Homer's complete logical reversal - rewarding the insult instead of the work ethic
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - gets sidetracked by ego and completely abandons his original point about work ethic

jokes.text: You're paying me in hair? Are you insane?
jokes.setup: Bart expects normal payment for work
jokes.punchline: Gets paid with literal hair instead of money
jokes.explanation: Simple absurd visual gag - the unexpectedness works but isn't particularly sophisticated

jokes.text: You take these. You hang Thai menu on door. I get more business. Send daughters to small liberal arts college - Swarthmore, maybe Sarah Lawrence. Call professors by first name. Hah!
jokes.setup: Simple menu hanging job
jokes.punchline: Apu's elaborate fantasy escalating from business success to very specific college aspirations
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation showing Apu's immigrant dreams through very specific liberal arts college details. The 'call professors by first name' is perfect specificity.

jokes.text: Hey, hey! No menus- Or I'll cut you like a box along the flaps!
jokes.setup: Threatening someone about hanging menus
jokes.punchline: The threat uses box-cutting terminology for human violence
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that transforms mundane box-cutting into a bizarre threat. Very quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: Now restaurant fail... children go to state college. Serious students powerless against drunken 'jockocracy.' Baseball hats everywhere!
jokes.setup: Apu lamenting his business failure
jokes.punchline: His horror escalates to very specific fears about state college culture
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that inverts his earlier dreams. 'Jockocracy' is an inspired word choice, and 'baseball hats everywhere' is the perfect final detail.

jokes.text: Menu boy no be coward like shrimp. Menu boy be brave like prawn.
jokes.setup: Apu giving motivational speech to Bart
jokes.punchline: Distinguishes between shrimp (cowardly) and prawn (brave) as if they're different creatures
jokes.explanation: Absurd distinction between nearly identical crustaceans as personality types. The broken English delivery adds to the character comedy.

jokes.text: Leave no footprint, only lunch specials.
jokes.setup: Ninja-style training advice
jokes.punchline: Subverts 'leave no trace' philosophy to promote restaurant business
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and wordplay. Takes mystical martial arts wisdom and corrupts it for commercial purposes. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: I don't know. A million? / You're ruining the earth! / True, but I 'gots' to get paid.
jokes.setup: Lisa's environmental outrage at Bart's tree waste
jokes.punchline: Bart casually admits to earth destruction but prioritizes money
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart - completely unbothered by moral implications when money is involved. The 'gots' adds character flavor.

jokes.text: Hey, some people in this family are doers, and some are 'don't-ers.'
jokes.setup: Bart defending his work ethic
jokes.punchline: Creates the nonsensical category of 'don't-ers' to categorize Lisa
jokes.explanation: Kid logic creating a binary world view. The made-up word 'don't-ers' is typical Bart vocabulary.

jokes.text: I knew this day would come. The cows are taking back what's theirs.
jokes.setup: Seeing cows on the Krusty Burger roof
jokes.punchline: Homer interprets it as a cow revolution rather than a protest
jokes.explanation: Homer's paranoid interpretation of protesters in cow costumes as actual bovine uprising. Good absurdist logic.

jokes.text: Get back in. It's only funny with a small car.
jokes.setup: Police trying to manage the crowd
jokes.punchline: Officer acknowledges the visual comedy physics of the gag
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about visual gag conventions - the officer understands comedy timing and scale requirements.

jokes.text: Take out the mother cow. The rest will follow.
jokes.setup: Police strategy for handling protesters
jokes.punchline: Wiggum applies livestock management to human protesters
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum - applying completely wrong tactical thinking. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: You can't silence the truth with beanbags! / That's nice work with the 'bag-zooka.' Gotta love what you do, Chief.
jokes.setup: Serious protest about truth and justice
jokes.punchline: Lou's casual appreciation for weapon wordplay and job satisfaction
jokes.explanation: The contrast between passionate activism and casual police banter. 'Bag-zooka' is a solid portmanteau.

jokes.text: All right, cow boy. I'll see you in 'moo-nicipal' court. / Good one, Chief. / What? What'd I say?
jokes.setup: Wiggum arresting protester in cow costume
jokes.punchline: Makes perfect pun unconsciously, then doesn't understand why Lou appreciated it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Wiggum moment - accidentally brilliant wordplay followed by complete obliviousness. Great character comedy structure.

jokes.text: He'd be cute if he weren't so idealistic.
jokes.setup: Looking at young environmental activist
jokes.punchline: Marge finds principles to be an aesthetic flaw
jokes.explanation: Marge's suburban middle-aged perspective where idealism is seen as an unfortunate character flaw. Good character insight.

jokes.text: The group is led by teenage activist Jesse Grass... a 'dreadlocked' dreamboat whose 'Birken stock' is on the rise.
jokes.setup: News report about environmental activist
jokes.punchline: Multiple puns connecting Birkenstock shoes to stock market terminology
jokes.explanation: Decent pun work but feels more like news anchor joke than character-specific humor. The Birkenstock/stock market connection is clever but obvious.

jokes.text: Ah, the Luftwaffe- The Washington Generals of the History Channel.
jokes.setup: Homer watching History Channel
jokes.punchline: Compares Nazi air force to the Harlem Globetrotters' permanent opponents
jokes.explanation: Brilliant analogy connecting WWII history to basketball entertainment. Shows how history programming makes Nazis seem like perpetual losers.

jokes.text: First comes love, then comes- Um- Damn it, I know this.
jokes.setup: Bart reciting the classic children's rhyme about Lisa and Jesse
jokes.punchline: Can't remember the next line of the universal playground chant
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bart being frustrated by forgetting something every kid knows. The curse word adds to his frustration.

jokes.text: Hey, I get enough flaming toilet paper thrown on me at home.
jokes.setup: Homer declining to visit prisoner with Lisa
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of domestic toilet paper attacks
jokes.explanation: Casual mention of bizarre domestic violence/pranks. The matter-of-fact delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: You do yoga? / Yeah, but I started before it was cool.
jokes.setup: Lisa trying to connect with Jesse
jokes.punchline: Jesse's immediate hipster response about doing things before they were popular
jokes.explanation: Classic hipster 'before it was cool' attitude. Good character establishment for Jesse's pretentious nature.

jokes.text: I'm a level-five vegan. I won't eat anything that casts a shadow.
jokes.setup: Lisa mentions considering veganism
jokes.punchline: Jesse's veganism has impossible requirements based on shadow-casting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of dietary restrictions to absurd levels. The shadow criterion is both specific and impossible. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Only at home? You mean you don't pocket-mulch? / Oh, it's so decomposed.
jokes.setup: Lisa mentions composting at home
jokes.punchline: Jesse reveals he carries rotting organic matter in his pockets as virtue signaling
jokes.explanation: Perfect environmental extremism satire. 'Pocket-mulch' is a great coined term, and his proud tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Well, we might have an opening at the poseur level.
jokes.setup: Lisa asking to join environmental group
jokes.punchline: Jesse offering entry-level position as fake environmentalist
jokes.explanation: Good satire of environmental hierarchy and Jesse's condescending attitude toward newcomers

jokes.text: No. I like to save the environment my own way. [Tires Squealing]
jokes.setup: Homer declining to join environmental group
jokes.punchline: Immediately demonstrates wasteful behavior with loud car acceleration
jokes.explanation: Classic ironic juxtaposition - Homer's environmental claim immediately contradicted by his actions. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: 50,000 for Thai menus. Daughter on wait list at Bennington.
jokes.setup: Apu bidding on logging rights
jokes.punchline: Still obsessed with his daughter's college aspirations from earlier
jokes.explanation: Good callback maintaining Apu's character obsession. The juxtaposition of environmental destruction and parental ambition works well.

jokes.text: Sold to the rich Texan. Yee-haw! [Whooping]
jokes.setup: Tree auction conclusion
jokes.punchline: Stereotypical wealthy Texan with over-the-top celebration
jokes.explanation: Basic stereotype humor without much sophistication. Functional but predictable character beat.

jokes.text: Not for a Phish concert, not even for Burning Man.
jokes.setup: Jesse explaining the commitment required for tree-sitting
jokes.punchline: References the two most tempting events for a hippie/environmentalist
jokes.explanation: Good cultural specificity - these would indeed be the most tempting alternatives for someone like Jesse. Shows character knowledge.

jokes.text: Look at these refugees. How about a smile? / They've undergone terrible hardships. / Well, moping won't make it better.
jokes.setup: Homer seeing refugee camp on TV
jokes.punchline: His solution to human suffering is better attitude adjustment
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer obliviousness to serious situations. His American optimism applied inappropriately to genuine suffering.

jokes.text: Marge, she's gonna narc on our stash. / We don't have a stash. / No. Of course not.
jokes.setup: Homer worried about Lisa's confession
jokes.punchline: Reveals paranoia about non-existent drug stash, then awkward backtracking
jokes.explanation: Homer's guilty conscience revealing things Marge didn't know about. The awkward recovery is well-timed.

jokes.text: Maybe I could just circulate a petition.
jokes.setup: Lisa realizes how high up in the tree she is
jokes.punchline: Immediately considers less dramatic alternative activism
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's second thoughts when faced with physical reality. Relatable fear response.

jokes.text: Oh, I didn't think it would be so high. Maybe I could just circulate a petition.
jokes.setup: Lisa looking up at tall tree she volunteered to live in
jokes.punchline: Immediate regret and suggestion of much easier alternative
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Lisa's idealism meeting practical reality, suggesting typical liberal alternative

jokes.text: Hang in there, Laura! / It's Lisa! / Right! Lisa. You're hard core.
jokes.setup: Jesse arriving to support Lisa's tree protest
jokes.punchline: Gets her name wrong, then covers with generic praise
jokes.explanation: Shows Jesse's casual attitude toward individual activists vs. the cause. The generic 'hard core' covers his mistake.

jokes.text: This is your fault, with your nonthreatening, Bobby Sherman-style good looks. / No girl could resist your charms. / I'm sorry. I wasn't listening. I was lost in your eyes.
jokes.setup: Homer blaming Jesse for Lisa's situation
jokes.punchline: Homer himself becomes attracted to Jesse mid-accusation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation where Homer proves his own point by falling under Jesse's spell. The Bobby Sherman reference adds character depth.

jokes.text: This is your fault, with your nonthreatening, Bobby Sherman-style good looks. No girl could resist your charms. I'm sorry. I wasn't listening. I was lost in your eyes.
jokes.setup: Homer blaming Jesse for Lisa's tree-sitting
jokes.punchline: Homer getting distracted by Jesse's attractiveness mid-complaint
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer moment. The Bobby Sherman reference is perfect, and his distraction mid-rant is brilliant character comedy.

jokes.text: Excuse me. That's 'littlest' tree hugger. And whether you love or hate her politics... you've gotta go gawk at this crazy idiot.
jokes.setup: News anchor making grammar correction
jokes.punchline: Immediately follows with insulting characterization of Lisa
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between professional standards (grammar) and unprofessional content (calling child an idiot). News media satire.

jokes.text: Excuse me. That's 'littlest' tree hugger. And whether you love or hate her politics... you've gotta go gawk at this crazy idiot.
jokes.setup: News report correcting pronunciation
jokes.punchline: Professional anchor calling subject 'crazy idiot' on air
jokes.explanation: Good media satire - news anchor dropping professional facade to mock subject while encouraging voyeurism

jokes.text: Mom is calling rescue agencies... Dad is building a giant ladder... but it is of poor quality.
jokes.setup: Family update letter to Lisa
jokes.punchline: Casual mention that Homer's rescue attempt is inadequate
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery about Homer's typical incompetence. Bart's matter-of-fact tone makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Ew. Someone's been marking their territory.
jokes.setup: Lisa climbing down from tree
jokes.punchline: Discovers animal (or human) urination evidence
jokes.explanation: Basic gross-out humor without much sophistication. Functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: We found her sleeping bag right here. Our working theory is that the lightning exploded her. / You don't get to say that! / Back to you, Kent.
jokes.setup: Field reporter explaining Lisa's disappearance
jokes.punchline: Kent objects to field reporter using his signature sign-off
jokes.explanation: Good media satire about territorial news anchor behavior. The absurd 'exploded her' theory adds to the humor.

jokes.text: I'm afraid it doesn't look good, Kent. We found her sleeping bag right here. Our working theory is that the lightning exploded her.
jokes.setup: Reporter explaining Lisa's apparent death
jokes.punchline: Absurd scientific theory delivered seriously
jokes.explanation: Great absurd pseudo-scientific explanation delivered with news gravitas - 'lightning exploded her' is memorable

jokes.text: I loved Lisa Simpson- Loved her like a shrub. And I would give anything to see her sweet face again.
jokes.setup: Jesse giving emotional tribute to supposedly dead Lisa
jokes.punchline: Compares his love to feelings for a plant
jokes.explanation: Perfect environmental extremist characterization - his love for humans is equated with plant life. Shows his priorities.

jokes.text: But in death, she will do more for our cause than she ever could have done in life.
jokes.setup: Jesse continuing his eulogy
jokes.punchline: Coldly calculates Lisa's martyrdom value over her living contribution
jokes.explanation: Dark but accurate satire of activist movements that sometimes value martyrs more than living supporters. Shows Jesse's cold calculation.

jokes.text: But in death, she will do more for our cause than she ever could have done in life.
jokes.setup: Jesse memorializing Lisa's environmental contribution
jokes.punchline: Callously noting death's superior propaganda value
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about activist movements exploiting martyrdom - Jesse's shallow opportunism revealed

jokes.text: That's why in memory of that poor little girl... I'm turning this entire forest into the Lisa Simpson Wilderness Preserve.
jokes.setup: Rich Texan who bought logging rights
jokes.punchline: Announces complete reversal due to guilt over supposed death
jokes.explanation: Ironic victory through supposed tragedy. The capitalist's guilty conscience saves the environment.

jokes.text: This family has had nothing but bad luck when it comes to farce.
jokes.setup: Marge objecting to Lisa pretending to be dead
jokes.punchline: References the Simpson family's history of comedic situations gone wrong
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on the show itself. Marge acknowledges their sitcom existence and history of complicated schemes.

jokes.text: [High-pitched Voice] Come in.
jokes.setup: Homer needs to hide Lisa's survival from Ned
jokes.punchline: Uses obviously fake feminine voice to disguise himself
jokes.explanation: Basic physical comedy. Homer's terrible disguise attempt is predictable but fits his character.

jokes.text: So, Bart, our school policy... is to give students in your situation straight A's. / Get out! What's the catch? / The tragic loss of your sister.
jokes.setup: Skinner offering Bart straight A's
jokes.punchline: The reward is specifically for family tragedy, which Bart had forgotten about
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about institutional responses to tragedy. Bart's excitement followed by grim realization works well.

jokes.text: So, Bart, our school policy... is to give students in your situation straight A's. / Get out! What's the catch? / The tragic loss of your sister.
jokes.setup: Skinner announcing academic benefit for Bart
jokes.punchline: Bart's excitement deflated by remembering the cost
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bart's initial excitement about grades undermined by tragic context

jokes.text: Homer, uh, booze is on the house... seeing as how Lisa is, um- Oh, how do I put this? Ridin' the midnight train to slab city.
jokes.setup: Moe offering Homer free drinks due to Lisa's death
jokes.punchline: Moe's elaborate euphemism for death using train/morgue imagery
jokes.explanation: Classic Moe - awkward but creative euphemism for death. 'Slab city' is particularly inventive and quotable.

jokes.text: Dear Moe, if anything should ever happen to me... I want you to tear up my dad's tab... and pour cocktail onions... down your pants.
jokes.setup: Homer reading fake final message from Lisa
jokes.punchline: The 'dying wish' escalates from practical to absurd punishment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from sensible request to bizarre demand. Homer's opportunism combined with cruel creativity.

jokes.text: Well, I ain't never said no to a dead girl yet.
jokes.setup: Moe considering whether to follow Lisa's 'dying wish'
jokes.punchline: Moe's twisted moral code about honoring the deceased
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe - twisted logic and questionable moral boundaries. Implies disturbing history while being oddly respectful.

jokes.text: Don't touch it! They can clone her from the spit.
jokes.setup: Branford Marsalis about to play Lisa's saxophone
jokes.punchline: Homer's misunderstanding of cloning science applied to grief
jokes.explanation: Homer's scientific misunderstanding combined with desperate parental hope. Good economy and character consistency.

jokes.text: the world's 'rootin'est,' 'tootin'est,' 'pollutin'est' amusement park!
jokes.setup: Rich Texan announcing new development plans
jokes.punchline: Rhyming scheme that ends with environmental destruction as a selling point
jokes.explanation: Good escalating rhyme that subverts expectations by ending with pollution as a positive. Fits Texas character.

jokes.text: [Apu] It was Lisa's dream.
jokes.setup: Rich Texan's absurd claim about Lisa's wishes
jokes.punchline: Apu's deadpan acceptance of obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat - Apu going along with obvious fabrication, economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: I'm not dead-And neither is my sense of moral outrage.
jokes.setup: Lisa dramatically revealing she's alive
jokes.punchline: Connects her survival to her continued activism
jokes.explanation: Good character-appropriate dramatic reveal. Lisa connecting her physical and moral existence is very much in character.

jokes.text: I don't get it. / What? / Oh, right- I don't have super powers... just yet.
jokes.setup: Lisa expecting the falling log to return to her
jokes.punchline: Realizes she mistakenly thought she had superhuman tree control
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lisa's occasional flights of fantasy. The 'just yet' suggests she still believes it's possible.

jokes.text: No! It was finger Ling-Ling good.
jokes.setup: Apu's restaurant being destroyed by rolling log
jokes.punchline: Plays on 'finger lickin' good' while his establishment is destroyed
jokes.explanation: Decent pun that works with the visual destruction. The wordplay on KFC slogan is appropriate for restaurant owner.

jokes.text: Yee-haw! Score one for the bad guys. [Laughs]
jokes.setup: Rich Texan watching environmental destruction
jokes.punchline: Openly celebrates being the villain and acknowledges his role
jokes.explanation: Character openly embracing villain status. Self-aware antagonist celebrating environmental destruction.

jokes.text: I even got 'em to install a solar-powered electric chair. / Dude, we've been here all morning. Could you at least re-moisten my head sponge?
jokes.setup: Jesse bragging about environmental improvements to prison
jokes.punchline: Death row inmate's practical complaint about execution delays
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor combining environmental activism with execution logistics. The inmate's practical concern is unexpectedly funny.

jokes.text: The song with multiple puns: 'This log is your log / When lightning struck it / It kicked the bucket / I poured some onions / Inside my trousers'
jokes.setup: Parody of 'This Land Is Your Land' about the log
jokes.punchline: Multiple callbacks to earlier events woven into folk song structure
jokes.explanation: Good ending callback song that ties together multiple plot threads. The folk song parody structure works well for the environmental theme.
